contract.name: v2/disaster/spending_by_geography.md
contract.description: Aggregates disaster emergency fund spending totals by state, county, or congressional district using DEFC-based filters and optional shared search constraints.

filter.description: Disaster filter object; must include at least one DEFC code and may include additional Search Filters v2 keys supported by this endpoint.

def_codes.description: Uppercase Disaster Emergency Fund codes identifying the disaster appropriations to aggregate.
def_codes.constraints: Minimum 1 item; values must match the published uppercase DEFC set (e.g., 'L', 'M', 'AAA'); lowercase entries are rejected with HTTP 400.

award_type_codes.description: Optional award type filters; accepts the same codes as Search Filters v2 (A, B, C, D, 02-11, IDV_*).
award_type_codes.constraints: Values outside the documented award type list trigger HTTP 400 validation errors.

time_period.description: Optional date ranges; accepted structures match the shared Search Filters v2 time period objects (YYYY-MM-DD strings).

items.description: Standard location object with at least a 'country' key; API accepted entries with {"country":"USA","state":"CA"}.

recipient_locations.description: Optional list of recipient geography filters following the standard location object schema.

geo_layer_filters.description: Optional allowlist of shape codes for the selected layer.
geo_layer_filters.constraints: If provided, must contain at least one string. Shape codes are case-sensitive; valid codes return matching rows, lowercase or unknown codes are silently ignored, and certain alphanumeric county codes (e.g., '34LL') caused the server to close the connection without a response.

scope.description: Selects whether to group by recipient location or place of performance.

geo_layer.description: Echo of the requested geographic layer.

scope.description: Scope used for aggregation; defaults to 'recipient_location' when omitted in the request.

spending_type.description: Echo of the requested spending type.

results.description: Array of aggregated totals for each returned shape; may be empty when filters exclude all data.

shape_code.description: Geographic identifier (state postal code, 5-digit county FIPS, or 4-digit state FIPS + district); null indicates the aggregate bucket when no geo_layer_filters are provided.

display_name.description: Human-readable geography label; may be null even when shape_code is populated.

amount.description: Aggregated dollar amount for the selected spending type; may be negative.

population.description: Associated population; often null for territories or the null aggregate bucket.

per_capita.description: Per-capita amount calculated from population; null when population is unavailable.

award_count.description: Count of awards included in the aggregated amount.

body.detail: Field 'filter|def_codes' value '[]' is below min '1' items

quirks: Omitting scope returns 'recipient_location' automatically and echoes it in the response.
Supplying geo_layer_filters removes the null aggregate row; without filters an extra null shape_code bucket contains uncoded totals.
geo_layer_filters matching is case-sensitive; lowercase or mistyped codes silently drop from the response, yielding empty results if none remain.
Requesting county geo_layer_filters with certain alphanumeric codes seen in full responses (e.g., '34LL') caused the server to close the connection without returning JSON.
Face value of loan spending currently reports zeros for named shapes while the null aggregate bucket can hold non-zero totals.
display_name, population, and per_capita may be null even when shape_code is present.
risks: County and district layers return very large result sets (hundreds of records, ~100KB bodies), which can stress clients and upstream services.
geo_layer_filters case-sensitivity means silent empty responses on typos, increasing the chance of unnoticed misconfiguration.
Connection closures observed when requesting certain alphanumeric county codes (e.g., '34LL') indicate potential WAF or stability issues requiring client retries.
Validation errors split between HTTP 400 and 422, so callers must handle both status families.
gaps: Shared filters beyond award_type_codes, time_period, recipient_scope, and recipient_locations were not exercised; compatibility is inferred from Search Filters v2 docs.
Unknown maximum payload size or safe upper bound for geo_layer_filters; large unfiltered requests occasionally preceded connection drops.
Unable to confirm whether error payloads include auxiliary fields (e.g., messages) because the server sometimes closed the connection before responding.

probes.notes: Baseline request: scope defaulted to recipient_location, null aggregate bucket present.
Changing spending_type alters amounts while retaining structure.
Applying geo_layer_filters removes the null aggregate bucket and shows extended fields including population and per_capita.
DEFC codes are case-sensitive; lowercase input rejected with HTTP 400.
Lowercase geo_layer_filters are silently ignored, producing an empty result set without errors.
Mixed geo_layer_filters silently drop invalid entries while returning rows for valid codes.
Empty def_codes arrays are rejected with HTTP 422.
Request using county filter '34LL' caused the server to close the connection without a JSON response (RemoteDisconnected).

mismatches: Endpoint documentation declares the request body schema as a string, but the live API requires a JSON object with the documented attributes.
gaps: Shared filters beyond award_type_codes, time_period, recipient_scope, and recipient_locations were not exercised; compatibility is inferred from Search Filters v2 docs.
Unknown maximum payload size or safe upper bound for geo_layer_filters; large unfiltered requests occasionally preceded connection drops.
Unable to confirm whether error payloads include auxiliary fields (e.g., messages) because the server sometimes closed the connection before responding.
risks: County and district layers return very large result sets (hundreds of records, ~100KB bodies), which can stress clients and upstream services.
geo_layer_filters case-sensitivity means silent empty responses on typos, increasing the chance of unnoticed misconfiguration.
Connection closures observed when requesting certain alphanumeric county codes (e.g., '34LL') indicate potential WAF or stability issues requiring client retries.
Validation errors split between HTTP 400 and 422, so callers must handle both status families.
